name: produce-the-phase-2-prod-20260513-1822
idea: Produce the Phase 2 prod cutover runbook at docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md for the live-update editor redesign (per the linked v6 redesign doc). Phase 1 will deliver the new editor in dev with publishing OFF; Phase 2 enables publishing, runs new + old editors side-by-side in prod for 24-48h, monitors for partial-publish failures via topics.publication_status='partial', renames old tables to _legacy_*, and schedules deletion in 2-4 weeks. The runbook must specify: exact publishing-flag-flip procedure with pre-flip preconditions, partial-publish detection SQL (the queries oncall would run), rollback procedure (how to flip the runner back to the legacy editor without rewriting state), the comparison-with-shadow-mode rubric (what specific signals from the Phase-1 shadow-mode logs mean cutover is safe versus 'go back and tune'), the table-rename migration SQL with correct ordering and constraint handling, and a decision tree for the 24-48h watch period — what observable symptom triggers what action (abort, hot-fix, accept). Assume Phase 1 lands per spec; deviations get a 'consult the doc' escape hatch, not detailed branching. The runbook is the operator's source of truth during cutover.

history.message: Plan output failed structural validation: Plan must include at least one step section (`## Step N:` or `### Step N:` under a phase).

flags_addressed.reason: §5 verification now targets Discord-send suppression — Branch A checks for absence of a legacy live_update_editor_runs row (scheduler-tick gate); Branch B checks legacy run row carries the suppression marker and no live-channel posts attributed to the legacy bot identity. The 'zero new live_update_feed_items rows' check is removed; §1 Important guardrails calls out per :651 that both editors keep writing to their own tables during the watch.

§9 step 6 (formerly Step 10.5) now cross-checks against docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223 and src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968 as the authoritative legacy-table set, and explicitly notes that docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md's 'Legacy Storage And Helpers' is about daily_summaries and is NOT the right target.
Same as FLAG-001 — §5 verification reframed to Discord-send activity with Branch A/B handling for the Phase-1 suppression mechanism.

§7 partial-publish trip-wire reworded to '2 cumulative across Phase 1 shadow + Phase 2 watch' with explicit :600 citation. §6 adds a cross-window tally query that sums partials in both dev (Phase 1 shadow) and prod (Phase 2 watch) since the soak start date. Trip-wire language is also re-stated in §1 Important guardrails and §11 footer.

§5 step 4 explicitly anchors verification on the next observed live_update_editor_runs row (status='completed' after the flip timestamp), not a fixed 15-minute window. Cadence rationale per :633 noted inline.

§5 now branches the verification shape on the suppression mechanism: Branch A (scheduler-tick gate, legacy editor never runs) checks for no new legacy run rows; Branch B (publish-call gate at _publish_accepted_candidates / _select_publishable_candidates) checks the suppression marker on existing legacy run rows plus absence of legacy-bot live-channel posts. Operator selects the branch based on what Phase 1 actually shipped.

§7 'lease contention' row split into three distinct symptoms with distinct actions: new-editor self-contention (HOT-FIX release lease + verify next tick), legacy writing to new tables (ABORT — isolation broke), and new+legacy run rows overlapping in same lease key (ABORT — lease misconfigured). Each row references the relevant lease key per :684.

§9 step 1 names a default migration-runner path: a new timestamped file in .migrations_staging/ (e.g. 2026MMDDHHMMSS_live_update_legacy_rename.sql), matching the existing repo convention confirmed by ls .migrations_staging/. A TODO(phase-1) covers alternates (Supabase dashboard SQL editor, psql-direct) if Phase 1 elects a different path; the SQL body is identical regardless.

§1 step 2 now carries an explicit 'Provisional until Phase 1 lands' banner under the H1 that enumerates the four TODO(phase-1) markers (toggle name+location, suppression mechanism, prod trace channel, migration-runner path) and tells the operator not to execute the procedure until those are resolved. §3 preconditions checklist adds 'Phase-1 TODOs resolved' as an explicit gate.

§9 step 1 now names the full hand-off chain explicitly: author in .migrations_staging/ → copy VERBATIM (filename unchanged) into /Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/ per .migrations_staging/README.md → apply via `supabase db push` → verify the row recorded in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations before treating as applied. §10 step 2 uses the same chain for the DROP TABLE block. Operator cannot write SQL into staging and assume it ran.

Replaced the v2 `topics GROUP BY environment` cross-window tally (which would return 0 dev rows by design and mislead the operator) with a prod-only SQL count plus an explicit instruction in §6 to add the Phase-1 shadow-attempted-publish partial count from Phase-1 shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed + shadow logs per :611-644), citing :611/:627/:633 (Phase 1 publishing OFF) for why dev `topics` has no `partial` rows. Trip-wire is now sum-of-two-sources. The Fast Triage `-- swap to 'dev'` annotation in §2/§3/§4 also notes the dev rows are diagnostic only.

§5 Branch B verification now names the Discord-side count (operator scrolls the live channel since the flip timestamp for posts attributed to the legacy bot identity) as the PRIMARY signal. The SQL suppression marker is demoted to a nice-to-have secondary check that is acceptable to skip if Phase 1 doesn't write one. A fifth TODO(phase-1) is added to the §1 banner for the new-editor / legacy bot identity distinction so the Discord-side count has a concrete anchor.

Same fix as issue_hints — §9 names the full chain (`.migrations_staging/` author → `supabase/migrations/` apply destination → `supabase db push` → verify in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`). §10 uses the same chain. The destination path is given as an absolute path per `.migrations_staging/README.md`.

§1 provisional banner now includes an explicit out-of-scope clause: 'if Phase 1 ships any mechanism other than Branch A or Branch B (hybrid, per-guild config branch, per-channel rollout, partial guild rollout), ABORT to the consult-the-doc escape hatch and do not improvise.' §3 preconditions adds 'Phase 1 shipped Branch A or Branch B (not a third mechanism)' as a gate. §7 decision tree adds a row: 'Phase 1 turns out to be a third mechanism → ABORT immediately; the runbook does not cover it.'

Already addressed in v2 (verification anchors on next observed `live_update_editor_runs` row with `status='completed'`, not a fixed window). v3 self-review pass re-verifies this in Step 13.9; no further change needed.
Already addressed in v2 via Branch A / Branch B branching. v3 reinforces by demoting SQL marker to nice-to-have under Branch B and naming Discord-side count as primary; no scope expansion.

Already addressed in v2 (lease symptoms split into three distinct rows: new-editor self-contention HOT-FIX, legacy writing to new tables ABORT, lease-key overlap ABORT). v3 self-review pass re-verifies in Step 13.11; no further change needed.

Already addressed in v2 via the provisional banner. v3 strengthens by listing five TODO(phase-1) items (adding bot identity), explicit third-mechanism abort clause, and the corresponding precondition gate and decision-tree row.

last_gate.rationale: Seven of the ten blocking flags are flagged "addressed" by the critique itself and are disputed below with citations to the specific plan revisions that resolved them. The remaining three open flags (`callers`, `scope`, `correctness`) are the same observation under three category labels: the runbook references a "legacy run-source" discriminator on `live_update_editor_runs` that doesn't exist in the schema today — a sixth Phase-1 dependency. These are accept_tradeoff per the brief's explicit "consult the doc" escape-hatch authorization; the plan's structural containment (provisional banner, 'Phase-1 TODOs resolved' precondition, 'third mechanism → ABORT' decision-tree row, default-ABORT footer) already traps the case before any flip, and the executor will add a sixth TODO(phase-1) inline at write time. Iteration Pressure shows zero addressed-then-reopened across all groups; the loop is finding sharper sub-framings, not bouncing fixes back. Time to execute.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Iteration 3, weighted score 10.5 → 11.5 → 13.5 (rising as the critic enumerates one more Phase-1 dependency per round: TODO count went 4 → 5 → 6). 10 flags resolved across iterations 1-3 with plan deltas 56.1% / 48.1% — the planner is responsive and the runbook's structural shape (Discord-side flip verification, next-tick anchoring, cross-window trip-wire sourced from Phase-1 telemetry, split lease symptoms, full migration hand-off chain, third-mechanism abort clause) is sound. Three open flags are the same observation (run-source discriminator gap) under three labels. Iteration Pressure shows FG-002 / FG-003 / FG-012 at iters=3 with addressed-then-reopened=0 — sharper sub-framings, not stuck churn. Preflight green; no overlap with escalated debt subsystems. Further iteration will yield TODO #7 without changing the doc's structural shape.
warnings: Executor should add a sixth TODO(phase-1) entry to the §1 provisional banner for the legacy/new run-source discriminator on `live_update_editor_runs` (column, metadata JSON key, or trigger-prefix convention — whichever Phase 1 chooses). The §3 'Phase-1 TODOs resolved' precondition then traps the case where Phase 1 forgets it.

Executor should add a one-line note alongside the §5 Branch-A verification: 'requires Phase-1-provided run-source discriminator (sixth TODO); if absent at flip time, treat as third-mechanism case and ABORT.'
Sentence-level edits only at write time. Do NOT add net-new sections — length budget ~400 lines.

settled_decisions.decision: Runbook is doc-only at docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md; no code or tests change.
Rename runs only AFTER the 24-48h watch; pre-rename rollback is a single toggle flip leaving topics rows intact (per redesign.md:596).

Phase-1-dependent unknowns remain as labeled TODO(phase-1) placeholders. The §1 banner enumerates at least six at execute time: toggle name + location, suppression mechanism (Branch A or Branch B), Branch-B suppression marker, new-editor / legacy bot identity, prod trace-embed channel, and legacy/new run-source discriminator on live_update_editor_runs.
settled_decisions.rationale: Brief authorizes 'consult the doc' escape hatch for Phase-1 deviations; enumerating each known dependency traps missed cases without exhaustively branching.

settled_decisions.decision: §5 verification targets Discord-send activity (Branch A: legacy run absent / Branch B: legacy run present + no legacy-bot posts in live channel) and anchors on the next observed live_update_editor_runs row, not a fixed time window.

Partial-publish trip-wire is two-sourced: Phase-1 shadow partials from shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed + shadow logs per :611-644), Phase-2 prod partials from `topics WHERE environment='prod'`. Cumulative ≥2 → ABORT + restore topic_publications. `topics WHERE environment='dev'` is NOT queried (vacuous per :611/:627/:633).

§9 names the full migration chain: author in `.migrations_staging/` (timestamped filename) → copy verbatim to `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` per `.migrations_staging/README.md` → apply via `supabase db push` (or Phase-1 alternate) → verify in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`. §10 DROP uses the same chain.

§9 cross-references docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223 and src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968 for the six legacy tables; docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md is NOT the cross-reference target.

If Phase 1 ships any third mechanism (hybrid, per-guild config, per-channel rollout, partial guild rollout, or missing run-source discriminator), the runbook directs ABORT — it does not branch into coverage for those shapes.
settled_decisions.rationale: Brief authorizes the consult-the-doc escape hatch over detailed branching.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
